response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong full-stack web and backend engineer with a long career in TypeScript, Node.js, and D-lang, but he is not a match for this role. His C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project, far short of the 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ required. He has no demonstrated experience in capital markets, fixed income, investment banking, or any adjacent financial-systems domain, and no mention of SQL Server, Oracle, Summit, Kafka, or other platform-specific tools called out in the job description.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) demonstrated consistently across multiple roles
Broad polyglot background (TypeScript, Java, DLang, C++, C) shows adaptability and low ramp-up risk on new languages
Active public speaker and workshop facilitator indicating solid communication skills
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~6 months professional + a thesis project) versus the mandatory 5–10 year requirement
Zero capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking domain exposure anywhere in the resume
No evidence of Windows-platform development, SQL Server/Oracle usage, or any of the desirable financial-infrastructure tooling (Summit, Kafka, MQ)
